title: Seasonal Spring Recipe: Lemon‑Herb Shrimp Skewers on a Gyro Pan
keyword: spring lemon herb shrimp skewers gyro pan
meta_description: Discover how to make spring lemon herb shrimp skewers using a vertical gyro pan – a quick, flavorful recipe perfect for outdoor grilling or indoor oven cooking.
html:
When the weather warms up, I love pulling out the Vertical Gyro Pan with Skewer for Oven to whip up a light, bright dish that feels seasonal without any fuss. These spring lemon herb shrimp skewers gyro pan style are crisp, juicy, and infused with fresh herbs, making them ideal for a weekend brunch or a quick weeknight dinner.

Why a Gyro Pan Is Ideal for Shrimp Skewers

Traditional grill grates can let tiny shrimp slip through, but a vertical gyro pan keeps each skewer upright, allowing even heat circulation around the whole piece. The stainless‑steel construction distributes heat uniformly, so the shrimp cook at the same rate, preventing over‑cooking on the outside while the interior stays raw. Because the pan is designed for oven use, you can achieve that charred, smoky flavor without firing up the backyard grill.

Ingredients for Spring Lemon‑Herb Shrimp Skewers

  • 1 lb large shrimp, peeled and deveined (tails on for easy handling)
  • 2 Tbsp extra‑virgin olive oil
  • Zest of 1 lemon plus 2 Tbsp fresh lemon juice
  • 2 tsp finely chopped fresh parsley
  • 1 tsp chopped fresh thyme
  • 1 tsp chopped fresh rosemary
  • 1 tsp garlic powder or 1 minced garlic clove
  • ½ tsp coarse sea salt
  • ¼ tsp freshly cracked black pepper
  • Wooden or metal skewers (if using wooden, soak 30 min in water)

These ingredients highlight the bright citrus notes of spring while the herbs add depth without overpowering the delicate shrimp.

Step‑by‑Step Preparation

1. Marinate the shrimp. In a large bowl, whisk together olive oil, lemon zest, lemon juice, parsley, thyme, rosemary, garlic, salt, and pepper. Add the shrimp and toss to coat evenly. Let the mixture sit for 10‑15 minutes – the acid from the lemon begins to gently “cook” the shrimp, enhancing flavor while keeping them tender.

2. Thread the shrimp onto skewers. Arrange 4‑5 shrimp per skewer, spacing them a little apart so heat can reach each piece. This is where the gyro pan shines; the vertical design holds the skewers steady, so you don’t have to worry about them rolling around.

3. Preheat the oven. Set your oven to 425 °F (220 °C). While it heats, place the Vertical Gyro Pan with Skewer for Oven on the middle rack. If you prefer a slightly charred finish, you can switch the oven to broil for the last 2‑3 minutes.

4. Cook the skewers. Lay the loaded skewers into the pan’s slots, making sure the shrimp face outward. Roast for 8‑10 minutes, turning once halfway through. The shrimp should turn opaque and develop a light golden edge.

5. Finish with a drizzle. Remove the pan from the oven and immediately drizzle a little extra lemon juice over the hot shrimp. The residual heat releases the aromatics, creating a fragrant, tangy glaze.

Serving Ideas and Pairings

The finished spring lemon herb shrimp skewers gyro pan recipe works beautifully in a variety of settings. Serve them over a simple arugula salad tossed with a citrus vinaigrette for a light lunch, or pair them with a side of couscous flavored with toasted pine nuts and dried apricots for a heartier dinner. If you’re entertaining, arrange the skewers on a platter with a few lemon wedges and a small bowl of garlic‑yogurt sauce for dipping.

Cleaning and Maintaining Your Gyro Pan

After the meal, let the pan cool to warm before washing. Stainless steel is dishwasher‑safe, but hand‑washing with a non‑abrasive sponge preserves the polished finish. For stubborn residue, soak the pan in warm, soapy water for a few minutes, then scrub gently. Dry thoroughly to prevent water spots, and store the pan in a dry cabinet to keep it ready for the next spring feast.

FAQ

Can I use frozen shrimp?

Yes, but thaw them completely and pat dry before marinating. Frozen shrimp release more water during cooking, which can affect the sear.

Do I need to pre‑heat the gyro pan?

Pre‑heating isn’t required, but a hot pan helps achieve a quick sear and reduces cooking time. You can place the pan in the oven while it pre‑heats for best results.

Is the gyro pan safe for broiling?

Absolutely. The stainless‑steel construction tolerates high heat, so you can finish the shrimp under the broiler for an extra charred edge.
